# UC-16.3.3 · First Contact Resolution Rate by Group

## Description

FCR indicates support efficiency and customer satisfaction. Tracking by group and category supports staffing and process improvement.

## Value

FCR indicates support efficiency and customer satisfaction. Tracking by group and category supports staffing and process improvement.

## Implementation

Ingest incident closure data with FCR flag. Compute FCR rate by group and category. Report on trend and groups below target. Use for training and process review.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ServiceNow/Service Desk TA, ITSM API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Incident resolution, first-contact resolution flag.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest incident closure data with FCR flag. Compute FCR rate by group and category. Report on trend and groups below target. Use for training and process review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="incident"
| where is_open=0
| stats count, sum(eval(if(first_contact_resolution=1,1,0))) as fcr_count by assignment_group, category
| eval fcr_rate=round((fcr_count/count)*100, 1)
| sort -count
| table assignment_group, category, count, fcr_count, fcr_rate
```

Understanding this SPL

**First Contact Resolution Rate by Group** — FCR indicates support efficiency and customer satisfaction. Tracking by group and category supports staffing and process improvement.

Documented **Data sources**: Incident resolution, first-contact resolution flag. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ServiceNow/Service Desk TA, ITSM API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="incident". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where is_open=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by assignment_group, category** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fcr_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **First Contact Resolution Rate by Group**): table assignment_group, category, count, fcr_count, fcr_rate


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (FCR rate by group), Table (group × category FCR), Line chart (FCR trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="incident"
| where is_open=0
| stats count, sum(eval(if(first_contact_resolution=1,1,0))) as fcr_count by assignment_group, category
| eval fcr_rate=round((fcr_count/count)*100, 1)
| sort -count
| table assignment_group, category, count, fcr_count, fcr_rate
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (FCR rate by group), Table (group × category FCR), Line chart (FCR trend).
